entry.title: Asian Review of Books: 21 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: Asian Review of Books: 21 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-21 01:10AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • “The Kushnameh: The Persian Epic of Kush the Tusked” by Iranshah ibn Abu’l-Khayr
  • “Celia, Misoka, I” by Xue Yiwei
  • New Book Announcement: “China’s Millennial Digital Generation: Conversations with Balinghou (Post-1980s) Indie Filmmakers” by Karen Ma
  • Jacques Offenbach’s “Pomme d’Api” at the French May: a Gallery
  • “Beethoven in Beijing: Stories from the Philadelphia Orchestra’s Historic Journey to China” by Jennifer Lin
  • New Book Announcement: “Akira Kurosawa and Modern Japan” by David A Conrad
  • Podcast with James Uden and Daniel Majchrowicz about “Worlds of Knowledge in Women’s Travel Writing”
  • “The Cabinet” by Kim Un-su
  • “The History and Culture of Iran and Central Asia: From the Pre-Islamic to the Islamic Period”, edited by DG Tor and Minoru Inaba
  • “Fish Swimming in Dappled Sunlight” by Riku Onda
In addition to reviewing books about or of relevance to Asia, the Asian Review of Books also features long-format essays by leading Asian writers and thinkers, to providing an unparalleled forum for discussion of key contemporary issues by Asians for Asia and a vehicle of intellectual depth and breadth where leading thinkers can write on the books, arts and ideas of the day. Widely quoted and referenced, with an archive of more than one thousand book reviews, it is the only web resource dedicated to Asian books. And now, with the addition of the new premium content, the Asian Review of Books, is a must-read publication.
